semester_info.notes: Course descriptions and syllabi can be found here.

design_core.name: Visual Design for HCI
design_core.delivery_mode: Online, asynchronous (No meeting time for Section 01 or 02)
design_core.description: Human interaction design as it applies to HCI. Aspects of audience analysis, design methodologies for creating concepts and solutions, techniques of concept prototyping, and the fundamentals of visual design such as color, type, symbolism, and grid structure. Class discussions, tutorials, and hands-on projects.

implementation_core.name: Python Application Development in HCI
implementation_core.registration_info: Registration Permission is Required: Please contact Tiffany Kayser
implementation_core.description: Implement Python code and write design documents ('specs') and complement their code with sufficiently detailed documentation in development of a large Python project of the student's choosing. Typical development process: idea, specification, prototyping and implementation (including debugging, testing, refactoring) and documentation and result in a demo-able final product.

phenomena_core.name: Design and Ethics
phenomena_core.description: Issues in ethics and decision-making as they relate to technology, design, design research, HCI, and the design industry.

recommended_electives.name: Models and Theories in Human Computer Interaction
recommended_electives.description: Survey of the multidisciplinary models and theories that form the foundation of the science of Human Computer Interaction. Application of the scientific method to solve practical problems by using analyses or approaches from the behavioral and social sciences, and information and computer technology

recommended_electives.name: Special Topics: Human Computer Communication
registration_instructions: Submit a Prerequisite Override task for this course in Workday
The instructor will approve your request
Contact Tiffany for questions or issues
recommended_electives.description: This course explores verbal and nonverbal communication theories and methods in AI and robotics research, focusing on human communication. Students will gain a deeper understanding of the research process in Communication Studies

general_electives.name: Advanced Visualization in Interior Design
general_electives.description: Exploration of advanced-level computer visualization techniques and applications relevant to design. Introduction to the latest graphic communication software and technologies, focusing on image searching, diagramming, hybrid digital perspectives, 3D modeling, photo-realistic rendering, real-time video production, and Virtual Reality. This course will follow a lecture format, with the instructor demonstrating various techniques. A Windows-based computer is required for the proper operation of the software. Each week, students will be introduced to new applications and software relevant to Interior Design, Architecture, Graphic Design, Landscape Architecture, Industrial Design, and Human-Computer Interaction. Students are expected to watch recorded lectures, complete five short-term tutorials as course exercises, and work on a long-term course project (Microhome or another topic) based on the knowledge acquired in this course.

general_electives.name: Workshop - Artificial Intelligence Tools for Designed Environments
registration_instructions: Submit a Prerequisite Override task for this course in Workday
The instructor will approve your request
Contact Tiffany for questions or issues
general_electives.description: This course explores the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) within designed environments, focusing on tools and technologies that enhance the design process from ideation to post-production. In addition to mastering AI tools, students will engage in responsible and design practices, including a written essay on the ethical use of AI in design. This course equips students with technical skills and the ability to communicate their design decisions critically.
